#tomatodalrecipe #lunchrecipes #tomatopappuandhrastyle #healthyrecipes #dalrecipe #tomatorecipes #homecookingshow 




📌 Full Recipe & Step-by-Step Guide: 👉


https://homecookingshow.in/recipe/andhra-style-tomato-pappu/

To Make Cooked Dal

Toor Dal - 1/2 Cup
Onion - 1 No.
Tomato - 3 Nos
Green Chilli - 3 Nos
Turmeric Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Red Chilli Powder - 1 Tsp
Salt - 1 Tsp
Tamarind Puree
Water


For Tempering

Ghee - 3 Tbsp
Urad Dal - 1 Tsp
Mustard Seeds - 1 Tsp
Cumin Seeds - 1 Tsp
Red Chilli - 3 Nos
Garlic - 5 Cloves
Hing / Asafoetida
Curry Leaves
Salt - 1 Tsp
Coriander Leaves


Method

To a pressure cooker add half cup of soaked door dal.
To this add chopped onions, chopped ripe tomatoes, green
chilies slit, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, rock salt and
mix it all together.
Add tamarind water to this and mix again.
Pour enough water for cooking and mix well.
Close the cooker, place the weight and cook till 4 whistles.
After 4 whistles, switch off the stove and wait for the pressure
to release.
Open the cooker and mash the cooked dal.
Keep the mashed dal aside.
To a pan, add ghee, urad dal. Mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red
chillies, slightly pounded garlic cloves and roast them.
Once the mustard seeds start spluttering, add asafoetida
powder, few fresh curry leaves and roast them.
Add the cooked and mashed dal and boil for 5 minutes.
Add salt if required and cook for some more time.
Add chopped coriander leaves and mix again.
Tasty tomato pappu is ready to be served nice and hot.

title: Dal Tadka Recipe | Restaurant Style Dal Tadka Recipe | Dhaba Style Dal Tadka Recipe | Varun Inamdar
description:
Learn How To Make Dal Tadka Recipe from The Bombay Chef - Varun Inamdar only on Rajshri Food. 
Make this simple, quick and easy Restaurant Style Dal Tadka Recipe at your home and share with us your experiences in the comments section below.

Ingredients:-
1 cup Toor Dal (Washed and soaked for 20 minutes and Boiled in 2 cups water and 1 tsp. salt)
1 tsp. turmeric powder

First tempering:
1 tbsp. ghee
¼ tsp. asafoetida
1 tbsp. ginger garlic green chilli, crushed
1 onion, finely chopped
1 tsp. Mustard seeds
1 sprig curry leaves
1 tomato, finely chopped
1 tsp. red chilli powder
1 tsp. coriander powder
Additional salt if required

Second tempering:-
1 tbsp. ghee
1 tsp. cumin seeds
1 tsp. red chilli powder
10 curry leaves
3 red chillies, dried

Coriander leaves

Method:-
- In a pressure cooker add the dal, turmeric powder, salt and water. Pressure cook until 7 whistles are blown.
- In a pan heat ghee, mustard seeds, curry leaves, onions, salt and saute until the onions are golden brown.
- Now add crushed ginger-garlic-green chilli, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, asafoetida and chopped tomatoes. Let it cook for 4-5 minutes.
- Transfer the pressure cooked dal into this masala and mix properly and add water.
- Add coriander leaves and turn the flame off.
- In a Tadka Waati or small utensil, heat ghee and add cumin seeds, curry leaves, whole dried red chillies, red chilli powder and pour hot over the dal.
- Mix thoroughly and serve hot!

Dal Tadka is ready to serve!

description:

Varan Bhaat Recipe | Rice Dal Recipe | Maharastrian Recipes | Lunch Recipes | Simple Varan Recipe

#varanbhaat #varanbhatrecipe #maharastrianrecipes #lunchrecipes #indianmaincourse #daltadka #marathirecipe #maharastrianvaranrecipe #easydalrecipe #dalfryrecipe #homecooking 

Prep Time: 5 mins
Cook Time: 30 mins
Servings: 4-5

To Cook Toor Dal

Toor Dal - 1 Cup
Turmeric Powder - 1/4 Tsp
Hing / Asafoetida Powder - 1/4 Tsp
Salt - 1/2 Tsp
Water

To Make Varan Bhaat

Ghee - 2 Tbsp
Mustard Seeds - 1/2 Tsp
Cumin Seeds - 1/2 Tsp
Hing / Asafoetida Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Garlic Cloves
Green Chilli - 2 Nos
Curry Leaves
Tomato - 1 No.
Jaggery - 1 Tsp
Coriander Leaves
Water
Salt

Method:

1. Wash and soak toor dal in water for 15 mins.
2. Transfer it into a pressure cooker and add water along with turmeric powder, asafoetida, salt.
3. Mix it all well, cover the cooker with a lid and cook the dal until 3 whistles on a medium flame.
4. To a pan, add ghee and then add in mustard seeds, cumin seeds, asafoetida powder.
5. Add in the pounded garlic cloves, green chillies and curry leaves.
6. After about half a minute, add in the tomatoes and cook for 2-3 minutes.
7. Then add in the cooked dal and water.
8. Mix it all well and add jaggery. 
9. Cook the dal for about 6-7 minutes and then add in the coriander leaves.
10. Yummy Varan Bhaat is ready to be served hot and nice with some steamed rice and papads by the side.

Varan Bhaat is a famous, comforting side dish recipe from the Maharashtrian cuisine. It is mainly made with dal and a very few other ingredients originally. But in this video, I have shown you all the way I enjoy it. So try this one sometime and enjoy it with hot steamed rice. This is also great for lunchboxes.

title: North Indian Home Style Toor Dal (Arhar Dal)
description:
Full Recipe - https://www.whiskaffair.com/toor-dal-recipe/

Ingredients 
To Cook The Dal
1 cup toor dal (arhar dal, split yellow pigeon peas)
½ teaspoon turmeric powder
1 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
1 tablespoon lime juice
For Tempering
2 tablespoons ghee (replace with oil for vegan)
2 teaspoons chopped garlic
¼ teaspoon asafetida (hing) (skip for gluten-free)
1 teaspoon cumin seeds
1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder

Instructions
Cook Toor Dal
In A Traditional Pressure Cooker
Wash dal 2-3 times with water until the water runs clear.
Soak it in 3-4 cups of water for 30 minutes.
Drain the water and add the soaked dal to a pressure cooker.
Add 3 and ½ cups of water, turmeric powder, and salt, and stir gently.
Close the lid of the cooker and cook the dal for 1 whistle over high heat.
Now reduce the heat to low and cook for 12 minutes.
Remove the cooker from heat and let the pressure release naturally.
Open the lid and whisk the dal well using a wire whisk. Add lime juice and mix well.
Add some boiling hot water if the dal is thick for your liking and mix well.
Check for salt and add more if needed.
In An Instant Pot
Wash dal 2-3 times with water until the water runs clear.
Soak it in 3-4 cups of water for 30 minutes.
Drain the water and add the soaked dal to an instant pot.
Add 3 cups of water, turmeric powder, and salt, and stir gently.
Close the lid of the instant pot and set the valve to the sealing position.
Press PRESSURE COOK and set the timer to 12 minutes at high pressure.
Once the timer goes off, let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes.
Release the remaining pressure manually and open the lid.
Whisk the dal well using a wire whisk. Add lime juice and mix well.
Add some boiling hot water if the dal is thick for your liking and mix well.
Check for salt and add more if needed.
Temper Arhar Dal
Heat ghee in a small pan over medium-high heat.
Once the ghee is hot, add garlic and fry until browned, stirring continuously.
Add asafetida and cumin seeds and let them crackle for 3-4 seconds.
Switch off the heat and add chili powder to the pan.
Pour the tempering immediately over the dal and mix well. Serve hot.

Notes
You can add some chopped tomatoes while cooking the dal.
This recipe requires a 6-quart instant pot or a 3-liter pressure cooker. If you want to double or triple the recipe, then use a bigger size pot or cooker.
Tempering Variations
Hing Jeera Red Chilli Tadka – You can just keep it simple and give the dal a tadka of only cumin seeds, dry red chilies, and hing in ghee.
It is great for people who are looking for a No Onion No Garlic version of this Dal.
Khatta Mitha Dal Tadka – Give arhar dal a tempering of ghee, hing, dry red chilies, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds (methi dana), cumin seeds, peanuts, tomatoes, red chili powder, curry leaves. Add tamarind pulp and grated jaggery and mix well.
Onion Green Chilli Tadka – Sometimes, I give my garlic tadka a break and add some finely chopped green chilies and thinly sliced onions to the tadka.
You can also add finely chopped garlic and ginger along with green chilies.
Tomato Dal Tadka – This is one of my favorite tadka to add to the toor dal.
You will need ghee, dry red chilies, hing, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves, finely chopped garlic, finely chopped onions, red chili powder, and chopped tomatoes.
Punjabi Dal Tadka – This is a simple tadka, which is made in many households.
For this one, you will need – ghee, cumin seeds, garlic, onions, tomatoes, and red chili powder.

description:

#masoordalcurry #redlentilcurry #dalchawalrecipe #lunchrecipes #lunchboxrecipes  #curryrecipe #daltadka #curryrecipe #homecooking #homecookingshow

Our Other Curry Recipes

Mushroom curry - https://youtu.be/rTLMj6FEo20
Dhaba style matar masala - https://youtu.be/KrNJvqqNgEY
Green gram curry - https://youtu.be/y9Xwu9q2S60
Bagara baingan - https://youtu.be/4Hk5eAofcyU

Prep Time: 5 mins
Cook Time: 45 mins
Servings: 4-5 

To Make Masoor Dal Curry

Masoor Dal - 1 Cup
Oil - 2 Tbsp
Cumin Seeds - 1 Tsp
Onion - 1 No.
Garlic - 2 Cloves
A Piece Of Ginger
Green Chilli - 3 Nos
Tomato - 3 Nos
Hing / Asafoetida Powder
Turmeric Powder -1/4 Tsp
Kashmiri Chilli Powder - 2 Tsp
Coriander Powder - 2 Tsp
Cumin Powder - 2 Tsp
Salt - 2 Tsp
Amchur Powder - 2 Tsp
Hot Water - 1 Litre
Garam Masala - 2 Tsp
Ghee - 2 Tsp
Coriander Leaves

Method:

1. Wash and soak the dal in water for about 1 hour.
2. Take a wide kadai and add oil to it. Once the oil is hot, add in cumin seeds and saute.
3. Add in the chopped onions and saute for 2-3 mins.
4. Add chopped garlic, ginger, cut green chillies and saute until the onions are golden brown in color.
5. Add in chopped tomatoes and saute for 1 min.
6. Then add hing/asafoetida powder, salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, amchur powder and mix it all well.
7. Once the tomatoes are mushy and oil begins to separate from the masala, add in the soaked masoor dal.
8. For cooking the soaked dal, add 1 litre of hot water into the kadai and cook the dal on a medium flame for about 30 minutes by covering the kadai with a lid.
9. After 30 minutes, add garam masala powder and check for seasoning. Add salt if required.
10. Mix it all well and add ghee, choriander leaves in the end. Give it all a quick mix.
11. Masoor dal curry is ready to be served hot with some hot rice by the side.

Dal/lentil based curries are very common in Indian food. We make different styles of dal in different places due to the difference in culture and cooking habits. There's dal with leafy vegetables, there's dal with vegetables like mango, cucumbers, tomatoes, brinjals, pumpkin, bottle gourd, ridge gourd etc in the south of India. In the north of India, the dal is a masala based curry most of the time except for a few recipes like Varan bhaat which are very traditional and close the native cooking. So this video is about Masoor Dal curry wherein the lentils are soaked and cooked in a masala gravy. This can be enjoyed with rotis or even plain rice because what beats the famous combination of Dal and Chawal? title: One Pot Dal | Simple, Comforting Indian Lentils (Masoor Dal Recipe)
description:
This is one of the simplest, most comforting things you can cook. A one-pot masoor dal that’s creamy, warming, and full of flavour, finished with a golden, fragrant tadka.

*NOTE: This water-to-dal ratio and timing apply to cooking on gas.

On induction or electric hobs, heat is applied mostly from the base rather than all around the pan, so water evaporates more slowly and the dal takes longer to thicken.

So if you are cooking this dal on induction or electric hobs, begin with 3.5-4 cups of water and extend the cooking time by 5–10 minutes. Check the consistency and add more water if required, adjusting to suit your heat source.


Ingredients:
 • 1 cup red lentils (masoor dal)
 • 5 cups water
 • 2 juicy tomatoes, chopped
 • 1½ tsp turmeric powder
 • 1 tsp homemade ginger-garlic paste
 • Salt to taste

Method:

Add all ingredients to a pot and bring to a rolling boil.
Lower the heat, cover, and let it cook gently for 20 minutes.
Stir and mash the lentils lightly for a smooth, creamy texture.

Tadka (Tempering):

In a small pan, melt a knob of butter.
Add:
 • 1 small red onion (or half, if large)
 • 4–5 garlic cloves, sliced
 • 1 tsp cumin seeds
 • A pinch of hing (optional)

Fry until golden and fragrant. Pour over the dal and cover for a minute to infuse.

Serve with:

Warm basmati rice, a simple raita, crunchy salad (tomatoes, onions, cucumber, lime, salt), and lime pickle on the side.

⸻

A bowl of this dal is like a warm hug.
If you enjoyed this recipe, don’t forget to like, subscribe, and leave us a comment!

title: Goan Dal Recipe | Popular Dal - Goa Style | Masala Trails With Smita Deo
description:
Learn how to make authentic Goan Dal at home from our Chef Smita only on Rajshri Food.

Goan Dal is the most basic and most loved dish in Konkan cuisine. This dal that originates from Goa – the most ‘touristy’ place in India has found its place in most of the popular restaurant menu. Try this simple and delicious dal at home and post your comments below.

INGREDIENTS

1 cup split green gram
5 green chillies slit
1 medium tomato chopped
2 pieces of kokum
Salt to taste

MASALA PASTE:

1 cup fresh coconut grated
3 shallots
1 tsp cumin seeds
3 pods of garlic
½ tsp turmeric powder

FOR THE TEMPERING:

1 tsp mustard seeds
1 tsp cumin seeds
Few curry leaves
4 pods of garlic crushed
3 dry red goan chillies broken
¼ tsp asafetida
2 tbsp coconut oil

METHOD:

 - Cook the pigeon peas along with the chillies, tomato and a cup of water.
 - Cook the drumsticks separately.
 - Grind all the ingredients under the masala paste with a little water.
 - Add the ground masala, kokum and the drumsticks to the cooked lentil and let it boil.
- Heat the coconut oil and crackle the mustard seeds, add the cumin and the garlic and let the garlic turn golden. Add the asafetida and the chillies along with the curry leaves and lace this dal with the temper and serve along with rice.

title: Dal Dhokli Recipe | Gujarati Special | स्स्वादिष्ट दाल ढोकली | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe for Dal Dhokli

Prep time: 10-15 minutes
Cooking time: 30-35 minutes (excluding soaking time)
Serves: 4-5 people

Dal
Ingredients:
TOOR DAL | तूर दाल 1 CUP
WATER | पानी 3 CUPS
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
PEANUTS | मूंगफली 2 TBSP
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP
MUSTARD SEEDS | राई 1/2 TSP
CLOVES | लौंग 3-4 NOS. 
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1/2 INCH
RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 2 NOS.
FENUGREEK SEEDS | मेथी के बीज 1/2 TSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ते 8-10 NOS. 
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 NO. (CHOPPED)
GINGER CHILLI PASTE | अदरक और मिर्च की पेस्ट 1 TBSP 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
JAGGERY | गुड़ 1 TBSP
TAMARIND PULP | इमली का पल्प 1 TBSP
Method:
Wash the toor dal well & soak it in fresh water for 30 minutes to an hour.
Once soaked, discard the water & add the dal into a pressure cooker & add water, turmeric powder & salt.
Place a steel bowl over the dal in the cooker & add the peanuts into the bowl, put the cooker’s lid on & cook the dal over medium high flame for 2-3 whistles, then switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize.
Once de-pressurized open the lid, remove the peanuts & set them aside, then blend the dal using a blender, your boiled toor dal is ready.
Now to temper the dal set a deep pan or a stock pot over high flame & once it gets hot add in the ghee & let the ghee heat as well.
Once the ghee gets hot add the cumin seeds, mustard seeds, cloves, cinnamon, red chillies, fenugreek seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves, tomato, ginger chilli paste & salt to taste, stir & cook over high flame until the tomatoes get slightly mushy.
Further add turmeric powder & kashmiri red chilli powder, stir quickly & add the boiled dal, stir well & add hot water to adjust the consistency of the dal, make sure to keep the dal a little watery at this stage as its going to simmer for a while you will be making the dhoklis.
Add the boiled peanuts, jaggery & tamarind pulp, stir well & set the flame to medium low, let the dal simmer until you prepare the dhoklis.
Dhokli
Ingredients:
WHEAT FLOUR | घेउं का आटा 1 CUP
GRAM FLOUR | बेसन 1 TBSP
SALT | नमक A PINCH
ASAFOETIDA | हींग A PINCH
CAROM SEEDS | अजवाइन 1/2 TSP (CRUSHED)
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
OIL | तेल 2 TSP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Method:
In a large bowl add all the dry ingredients along with the oil & mix everything well.
Further add water as required to make a semi-soft dough, the dough should be of the same stiffness as a puri/chapati dough.
Once you have kneaded the dough, cover it with a damp cloth for 10-15 minutes.
Divide the dough into 2 portions once rested, coat one portion with dry flour & roll it into a large chapati, it shouldn’t be too thick or too thin.
Further cut the chapati into squares, diamonds or strips using a pizza cutter or a knife, I have cut them into diamonds.
Cut the second portion similarly & your dhoklis will be ready to be added into the dal.
Final cooking
Ingredients:
TAMARIND PULP | इमली का पल्प 1 TBSP
JAGGERY | गुड़ 1 TBSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया AS REQUIRED (CHOPPED)
Method:
Check the dal that has been simmering, taste & adjust accordingly, I added a tablespoon of both tamarind pulp & jaggery, stir well & bring the dal to a boil before adding the dhoklis or else the dhoklis will stick to each other when you add them in the dal.
Once the dal comes to a boil, add the all the cut dhoklis carefully & then gently stir them into the dal.
Cover the pan with a lid & cook over medium low flame for 10 minutes.
Once cooked, remove the lid, you will see that the dal has thickened up a bit & the dhoklis will have fluffed up.
Add some freshly chopped coriander & stir well.
Your dal dhokli is ready, add ghee & lemon juice over the dal while serving along with crispy papads.

#dalkhichdi #ricerecipes #moongdalkhichdi #khichdirecipe #dietrecipes #restaurantstyledalkhichdi #lunchrecipes #lunchboxrecipes #homecookingshow 

Prep Time: 5 mins
Cook Time: 30 mins
Servings: 3-4

To Cook Dal & Rice

Basmati Rice - 1/2 Cup
Moong Dal - 1/2 Cup
Water 
Salt - 1 Tsp
Turmeric Powder - 1/2 Tsp

To Make Dal Khichdi

Oil - 1 Tbsp
Ghee - 1 Tbsp
Cumin Seeds - 1 Tsp
Bay Leaf
Cinnamon
Chopped Ginger
Chopped Garlic
Green Chilli - 1 No.
Onion - 1 No.
Tomato - 1 No.
Salt - 1/2 Tsp
Chilli Powder - 1 Tsp
Cumin Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Coriander Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Hing / Asafoetida - 1/4 Tsp
Garam Masala - 1/2 Tsp
Coriander Leaves

Method:

1. Wash and soak rice in a bowl for 30 mins.
2. Wash and soak moong dal in a bowl for 30 mins.
3. After 30 mins, transfer the soaked rice and dal to a pressure cooker. Add water, salt and turmeric.
4. Cook the dal and rice for 3-4 whistles on a medium flame and keep it aside.
5. Take oil and ghee in a pan.
6. Add cumin seeds, cinnamon, bay leaf and saute. Add chopped ginger and garlic, saute.
7. Add in the chopped green chillies and onions. Saute until they are slightly brown.
8. Then add in the tomatoes and cook for a min.
9. Add salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, hing and garam masala. Mix well.
10. Add in the cooked rice and dal mixture. Mix it all again.
11. Finally, add some ghee and chopped coriander leaves.
12. Comforting Dal Khichdi is ready to be served hot with fryums or papads, pickle by the side.

Hey guys, 

I am gonna show you all a lovely recipe today. It's Dal Khichdi. It's a soul comforting food for many because it is mild, subtle yet tasty and flavourful. This is basically a one pot dish that you can make quickly whenever you're not in a mood to cook or whenever you feel low and still have to cook and eat something. This is extremely soothing for your taste buds and you can have it as it is or with any pickle of your choice or papads by the side. Do try this yummy recipe and enjoy!

title: Dal Soup For Weight Loss | Lentil Soup Recipe | Delicious Vegetable Soup | Healthy Soup Recipes
description:
Dal Soup For Weight Loss | Lentil Soup Recipe | Delicious Vegetable Soup | Healthy Soup Recipes 

#dalsoup #weightlosssouprecipe #healthysouprecipe #vegsoup #lentilsouprecipe #homecookingshow #homecooking 


Other soup recipes

Carrot Beetroot Soup - https://youtu.be/E4WSB0D3exM
Tomato Soup - https://youtu.be/Duc89AZY9j4
Chicken Clear Soup - https://youtu.be/7iAzrcjSmLI
Mushroom Soup - https://youtu.be/t6gGmy-q_ss

Prep Time: 10 mins
Cook Time: 35 mins
Servings: 4

To Make Dal Soup

Toor Dal - 2 Tbsp
Masoor Dal - 2 Tbsp
Moong Dal - 2 Tbsp
Ghee - 3 Tsp
Garlic - 2 Cloves
Ginger
Green Chilli - 2 Nos
Onion - 1 No.
Tomato - 1 No.
Pumpkin - 1 Cup
Carrot - 1 No.
Turmeric Powder - 1/4 Tsp
Pepper - 1 Tsp
Cumin Powder - 1 Tsp
Kashmiri Chilli - 1 Tsp
Coriander Leaves
Salt
Water

Method:

1. Take toor dal, masoor dal and moong dal in a bowl.
2. Wash them nicely and soak them in water for about 10 mins.
3. Take ghee in a pressure cooker.
4. Add chopped garlic, ginger, green chillies and onions to it. Saute until the onions are pink.
5. Then add in chopped tomatoes, pumpkin and carrot.
6. Saute the vegetables for a few mins.
7. Add in the soaked dal, turmeric powder and salt. Mix well.
8. Add 2 cups of water to the cooker and mix. Pressure cook the dal & veggies for 3-4 whistles on a medium low flame.
9. Cool the cooked dal and vegetables. Transfer them into a mixer jar, add water and grind into smooth paste.
10. Transfer the ground paste into a pan and add water. Mix.
11. Add in pepper powder, cumin powder and red chilli powder. Add salt if required. 
12. Boil the mixture for 5 mins on a medium low flame.
13. Add in coriander leaves and cook for 2 more mins. Add water if required/
14. Mix it all well.
15. Healthy and wholesome dal soup is ready to be served hot and nice.

Soups are one of the most wholesome dishes when proper ingredients are used. They are also easy to make. This Dal soup is one such nourishing whole meal. It has three varieties of dal and nutritious vegetables in it. For this recipe, the first step is to cook all the vegetables and dal. Then we have to prepare all the cooked ingredients into fine soup and season it. This video is an easy step-by-step guide to take you through the process without any confusion. So do watch the video till the end to see tips and tricks to make this delicious dal soup easily at home. You can have this soup whenever you want a little detox or whenever you are sick. You can also send this in lunchboxes with kids for their break time. Do try this recipe and enjoy with your family and friends.

title: Palak Dal Recipe - Dhaba Style Dal Palak - Spinach & Lentil Curry - Varun
description:
Learn how to make Palak Dal at home with Chef Varun Inamdar on Rajshri Food.

Palak Dal or Dal Palak is a simple lentil curry with spinach. This Dal recipe is quick and easy to make and tastes best when had with steam rice. Do try it at home and share your feedback in the comments below.

Visit our Website for more Awesome Recipes
http://rajshrifood.com/

Ingredients:
1 Onions, chopped
3 Green Chillies, chopped
1 inch Ginger, chopped
2 tbsp Ghee
1 Tomato, chopped
1 tbsp Cumin seeds
1/2 tsp Asafoetida
1 tsp Red Chilli powder
1/2 tsp Turmeric powder
1 tbsp Coriander seed powder
1 cup Lentil/Toor Dal, boiled
Salt
2 cups Spinach leaves, chopped
 
Method of preparation:
Heat a pan, add ghee and allow it to heat.
Add cumin seeds, asafoetida, onion, chilli, ginger, saute it on high flame till the onions turn translucent.
Add red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, dal, tomatoes, salt and give it a good mix.
Add spinach, mix well an let it cook.
Our Palak Dal is ready to serve.

title: Urad dhal (Black Lentil) Recipes from Sri Lanka 🫘 Indian and Sri Lankan Dosai, Sambaru & More!
description:
I’m harvesting the urad bean, also known as black gram and many other names, and will show you the journey these beans make from the field to your plate. (Or banana leaf!)
➡️ I’ll prepare today’s dishes in a traditional way: soaking and removing the skin, creating a bean paste on a stone grinder, and cooking over a wood fire on a clay stove.
Today’s menu consists of chili shrimp ulundu vadai, sambar, crispy dosa, and more!

Urad bean is related to mungo and is popular throughout South Asia.
➡️ Not only can it be used in soups like sambar, a vegetable mix with curry and tamarind, but it also goes well with sweeter foods like dosa, which is like a lentil-based pancake.
Join me for black gram-based meals that are good for your health and watch a cooking process that is also gentle on the environment!

title: Four Easy & Delicious Dal Recipes | Dal Recipes By Cook With Lubna
description:
In this video, I have shared 4 easy and delicious dal recipes that you can make at home with very simple ingredients. These dal recipes are perfect for daily cooking and taste absolutely amazing!

Recipes included:

Moong Masoor Dal
Kali Masoor Dal
Sabut Moong Dal
Instant One-Pot Dal Fry

All recipes are beginner-friendly, quick, and full of flavor. You don’t need complicated steps to make tasty food — these dals prove that simple cooking can give the best results.
Perfect to serve with steamed rice, jeera rice, or roti.
If you enjoyed the video, don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe ❤️
Try these recipes and share your feedbacks!


1. Moong + Masoor Dal (Mixed Lentils)

Today we’ll make a very tasty moong and masoor dal using a super simple method. The result will be so delicious that you won’t believe it was made this easily.
Ingredients:
¾ cup (150 g) red masoor dal (split, without skin)
¼ cup (50–60 g) moong dal (split, without skin)
3 cups (≈800 ml) water
2 medium tomatoes (finely chopped)
1 tsp salt (adjust to taste)
½ tsp red chili powder
Slightly less than ½ tsp turmeric
1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
3–4 green chilies (chopped)
Fresh coriander leaves
Method:
Wash both dals thoroughly 3–4 times. No soaking needed.
Add to a pressure cooker with water, tomatoes, salt, chili powder, turmeric, and ginger-garlic paste.
(Optional) Add 1 tbsp oil to prevent overflow.
Cook for 2 whistles, then let pressure release naturally.
Open, adjust consistency with water if needed.
Boil on high flame, then simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Add green chilies and coriander.
Tadka (Tempering):
Heat 2 tbsp desi ghee
Add ½ tsp cumin seeds
Add chopped garlic (2–3 cloves), fry till golden
Pour over dal, cover for 1–1.5 minutes
👉 Dal is ready — super aromatic and delicious!

🍲 2. Kali Masoor Dal (Whole Brown Lentils)
A very simple yet flavorful dal.
Ingredients:
1 cup (200 g) whole masoor dal (with skin)
4 cups water (5 cups if not soaked)
1½ tsp salt
½ tsp red chili powder
½ tsp turmeric
1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
1 tsp amchur (dry mango powder)
Fresh mint leaves
Green chilies
Method:
Soak dal for 30 minutes (optional).
Pressure cook with spices and water:
1 whistle on high, then 5–6 min on low flame
Let pressure release, adjust consistency.
Add amchur powder and boil again.
Add mint and green chilies.
Tadka:
Heat 2–3 tbsp ghee
Add garlic + 2 dry red chilies
Fry till golden and pour over dal
Cover for 1–2 minutes


🍲 3. Sabut Moong Dal (Whole Green Gram)
Healthy, nutritious, and easy to digest.
Ingredients:
1 cup whole moong dal (soaked 2–3 hours)
4 cups water
Salt, turmeric
2 bay leaves, 1 small cinnamon stick
Method:
Pressure cook:
1 whistle high flame, then 8–10 min low flame
Prepare masala in pan:
Oil + cumin + hing
Add onion, green chilies
Add ginger-garlic paste
Add spices + tomatoes, cook till soft
Add cooked dal and mix
Adjust water, boil well
Simmer 3–4 minutes
Final Tadka:
Ghee + onion + dry red chilies + julienned ginger
Pour on top
Add garam masala + coriander

🍲 4. Instant Dal Fry (One-Pot Recipe)
Perfect for quick cooking — everything in one cooker!
Ingredients:
¼ cup ghee
Dry red chilies
Hing + cumin
Onion (small)
Ginger + garlic
Green chilies
Spices (salt, chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder)
2 tomatoes
1 cup toor dal (soaked 30 min)
3 cups water (4 if not soaked)
Method:
Heat ghee in pressure cooker
Fry red chilies (remove and keep aside)
Add hing, cumin, onion, ginger, garlic, green chilies
Add spices + a little water
Add tomatoes and cook till soft
Add dal and roast lightly
Add water and pressure cook:
1 whistle + 2–3 min low flame
Open, adjust consistency, boil once
Crush fried red chilies and add
Add coriander
👉 Optional: Add extra tadka for richer taste
Final Tip:
All these dals are simple but extremely flavorful. You don’t need complicated steps to make delicious food — even easy methods can give amazing results.
Serve with:
Steamed rice 🍚
Jeera rice
Roti / chapati

description:

Dal Dhokli Recipe | Gujarati Dal Dhokli | No Onion No Garlic | Healthy recipes | Easy Dal Recipe

📌 Full Recipe & Step-by-Step Guide: 👉https://homecookingshow.in/recipe/dal-dhokli/

#daldhoklirecipe #gujaratidaldhokli #noonionnogarlicrecipes #easydalrecipe  #homecookingshow 

To Cook Dal

Toor Dal - 1/2 Cup
Turmeric Powder - 1/4 Tsp
Hing / Asafoetida
Salt - 1/2 Tsp
Peanuts - 1 Tbsp
Water - 2 Cups


For Tempering

Oil - 1 Tbsp
Ghee
Mustard Seeds -1 Tsp
Cumin Seeds - 1 Tsp
Red Chilli - 2 Nos
Green Chilli - 2 Nos
Hing / Asafoetida
Curry Leaves
Tomato - 2 Nos
Turmeric Powder - 1/4 Tsp
Chilli Powder - 1 Tsp
Coriander Powder - 1 Tsp
Salt - 1/2 Tsp
Jaggery



To Make Dhokli

Wheat Flour - 1 Cup
Turmeric Powder 
Chilli Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Cumin Powder -1/2 Tsp
Salt - 1/4 Tsp
Ajwain - 1/2 Tsp
Oil - 2 Tsp
Water

Prep Time: 20 mins
Cook Time: 40 mins
Servings: 3-4

METHOD:

For making the Dal

1. Wash and soak toor dal in water for 1 hour.
2. Transfer the soaked dal with water to a pressure cooker and add salt, turmeric powder, hing, peanuts. Add some more water, mix well and pressure cook on medium flame for 4-5 whistles.
3. Add some more water, mix well and pressure cook on medium flame for 4-5 whistles. Keep the cooked dal aside.
4. Take ghee and oil in a kadai. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and saute.
5. Once the mustard seeds start splattering, add red chillies, green chillies, hing and curry leaves.
6. Then add tomatoes and mix well. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder and mix well.
7. Add salt and cook the tomatoes till they are mushy. Then add the cooked dal on a medium low heat.
8. Mix well. Cover and cook for 5 mins on a medium flame.
9. After 5 mins, add jaggery for balancing the flavors. Mix and keep the dal aside.

For making the Dhokli

10. Take wheat flour, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, cumin powder, salt, ajwain in a large bowl.
11. Mix well and add some oil. Mix again. Now add water and prepare a nice dough.
12. Knead the dough for a minute or two. Cover and let it rest for 10 mins.
13. After 10 mins, divide the dough into small portions and roll out into medium thickness rotis.
14. Cut the rolled out dough into diamonds with a pizza cutter or a knife. Kepe them aside.

For the Dal Dhokli

15. Now heat the dal once again and add more water if it is thick.
16. Gently drop the prepared dhoklis into the dal. Cover and cook for 15 mins on a medium flame.
17. Affter 15 mins, the dhoklis will be nicely pumped up.
18. At this point, sprinkle some finely chopped coriander leaves and turn off the heat.
19. Delicious Dal Dhokli is ready to be served hot and nice. Sprinkle some lemon juice for an added flavour before serving.

Dal Dhokli is a Gujarati dish which can be called an Indian version of pasta made mainly with lentils and wheat flour. A lentil base of soupy consistency is made and wheat flour pasta is cooked in it. This dish is native to Rajasthan and Gujarat in India. This is enjoyed for breakfast or lunch or even dinner. Dal dhokli is a flavorful dish and if you are first timer, follow the exact steps to get the right taste. Watch this video till the end to get a step by step process on how to make dal dhokli at home. Try the recipe and let me know how it turned out for you guys in the comments below.

title: Simple and Delicious Masoor Dal Tadka & Ghee Pulao Recipe | झटपट और स्वादिष्ट रेसिपी
description:
Simple and Delicious Masoor Dal Tadka & Ghee Pulao Recipe | झटपट और स्वादिष्ट रेसिपी

Subscribe to my channel here : https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjC5zJ25kXB0mwKxi4erOrg

Learn how to make a comforting and delicious Masoor Dal Tadka and Ghee Pulao with this easy-to-follow recipe! Perfect for lunch or dinner, this combination of protein-packed dal and aromatic pulao is a crowd-pleaser. Get step-by-step instructions and cooking tips to bring authentic flavors to your table. Try it today and enjoy a wholesome, flavorful meal!

Portion servings: 5-6 pax
Preparation time: 15 mins
Cooking time: 50-60 mins

Ingredients:

For masoor dal tadka:
Ghee 1 tbsp 
Jeera 1 tsp
Garlic chopped 1 tbsp
Onion chopped 2 pcs
Ginger chopped 1 tsp
Green chilli chopped 1 tsp
Tomato chopped 2 pcs
Turmeric powder ½ tsp
Red chilli powder 1 tsp
Salt 1 tsp
Masoor dal soaked 1½ cup
Water 3 cups

For tadka:
Ghee 2 tbsp
Dry red chilli 2 pcs
Hing ½ tsp
Jeera ½ tsp
Red chilli powder ½ tsp
Coriander chopped 1 tbsp

For ghee pulao:
Ghee 1 tbsp
Bayleaf 1 pc
Mace 1 pc
Green cardamom 5 pcs
Cinnamon 1 inch
Cloves 6-7 pcs
Black pepper 10-12 pcs
Basmati rice soaked 1½ cup
Water 2¾ cups
Salt ½ tsp

Method:

For Masoor Dal Tadka
Step 1: Prepare the Dal Base
Heat ghee in a pan and add cumin seeds. Let them crackle.
Add chopped garlic and sauté until it turns golden.
Add chopped onions and cook until they become soft and translucent.
Stir in chopped ginger and green chilies, sauté for a few seconds.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften and blend into the mixture.

Step 2: Cook the Dal in the Pressure Cooker
Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt to the pan. Mix well.
Add soaked masoor dal to the pan and stir to combine with the spices.
Transfer the dal mixture to the pressure cooker. Add water and give it a quick stir.
Close the lid of the pressure cooker and cook on medium heat for about 3-4 whistles or until the dal is soft and cooked.
Once done, release the pressure and check the dal's consistency. Add more water if needed to adjust it to your liking.

Step 3: Prepare the Tadka
In a small pan, heat ghee. Add dry red chilies, a pinch of asafoetida (hing), and cumin seeds. Let them sizzle.
Add a little red chili powder and immediately pour the tadka over the cooked dal.
Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves and mix gently.

Step 4: Serve
Serve the dal hot with the flavorful tadka.

For Ghee Pulao

Step 1: Prepare the Spice Base
Heat ghee in a deep pan.
Add bay leaf, mace, green cardamoms, cinnamon, cloves, and black peppercorns. Sauté until aromatic.

Step 2: Cook the Rice
Add soaked basmati rice to the spiced ghee and stir gently to coat each grain.
Pour water into the pan and add salt.
Bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and let the rice simmer until fully cooked and fluffy.

Step 3: Serve
Fluff the rice gently with a fork and serve alongside the dal.

title: मेहमानों को जरूर खिलाएं ये वाली दाल | Mixed Dal Recipe | Dal recipe Mix dal Recipe| Keoti Dal Reicpe
description:
"This mixed dal tadka is super healthy and easy to make. 5 types of dal namely masoor dal, tur dal, moong dal, chana dal, and urad dal are soaked and then pressure cooked with tomatoes, green chilies and onion along with salt and turmeric. 

After that, a tadka made of ghee and red chilies is added that makes this recipe mouth-watering!


Health benefits: These lentils/dal are high in vitamin B, zinc, potassium and magnesium. This मिक्स्ड दाल तड़का is highly rich in protein.". 

This dhaba-style mixed dal tadka is perfect for those days when you're craving for something delicious and effortless. MIXED DAL TADKA

In this Unique show Master Chef Sanjeev Kapoor will teach the nation his top recipes. From main course to starters, it will feature his best signature dishes. Fresh and classy, this show continues to warm the hearts and homes of millions of people across India.



#MixedDalTadka #मिक्स्डदालतड़का #Alldalrecipes #PanchmelDalRecipe #sanjeevkapoorkhazana

Ingredients

¼ cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked for 3-4 hours and drained
¼ cup Bengal gram (chana dal), soaked for 3-4 hours and drained
2 tbsps whole black gram (sabut urad dal), soaked for 3-4 hours and drained
2 tbsps whole red lentil (sabut masoor), soaked for 3-4 hours and drained
¼ cup split skinless red lentil (dhuli masoor dal), soaked for 3-4 hours and drained
¼ cup pigeon peas (toor dal), soaked for 3-4 hours and drained
1 large onion, roughly chopped
1 medium tomato, roughly chopped
3-4 green chillies, slit
Salt to taste
½ tsp turmeric powder
A pinch of red chilli powder
Tempering
2 tbsps ghee
1½ tsps cumin seeds  
1 inch ginger, finely chopped
5-6 garlic cloves, crushed
¼ tsp asafoetida (hing)
2-3 dried red chillies, broken
¼ cup chopped fresh coriander leaves
Salt to taste
Fresh coriander sprig for garnish
To serve
Steamed rice as required

Method

1. Heat a pressure cooker, add split skinless green gram, Bengal gram, whole black gram, whole red lentil, skinless red lentil, pigeon peas, onion, tomato, green chillies, salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder and 4 cups water. Cook for 3-4 whistles. 
2. Open the cooker once the pressure has reduced completely. 
3. To make the tempering, heat ghee in a non-stick pan. 
4. Add cumin seeds and let them change colour. Add ginger, garlic, asafoetida, and dried red chillies and mix well. 
5. Add coriander leaves and mix well. Add the cooked gram mixture, mix and add 1 cup water and adjust salt, mix well and cook for 1 minute.
6. Garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Dal Mughlai Recipe | Rich Restaurant Style Dal Recipe | शाही दाल मुगलई | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe for Dal Mughlai

Prep time: 15-20 minutes (excluding soaking time)
Cooking time: 1 hour
Serves: 5-6 people

Ingredients:
Boiled Dal
GREEN MOONG | हरी मूंग दाल DAL 1 CUP
WHOLE SPICES
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 4 NOS. 
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 2 NOS.
CINNAMON STICK | दालचीनी 1 INCH 
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS. 
CLOVES | लौंग 2-3 NOS. 
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च 3-4 NOS. 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
WATER | पानी 1 INCH ABOVE THE DAL
Tempering
OIL | तेल 2 TBSP
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 3 NOS. (CHOPPED)
GARLIC | लेहसुन 2 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GINGER | अदरक 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
POWDERED SPICES
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/4 TSP
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TBSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
CURD | दही 3/4 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
Birista
OIL | तेल 1/2 - 3/4 CUP
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED (SLICED)
Final cooking
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम 2-3 TBSP
BUTTER | मक्खन 2 TBSP
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी हुई कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A LARGE PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Transfer the dal into a large bowl & wash it using a fresh water a couple of times until the added water stays clear, then cover it let it soak for 6-8 hours.
Take small muslin cloth & add green cardamom, black cardamom & all the remaining whole spices & tie it up.
Discard the water from the dal & add the soaked dal into a pressure cooker, add the whole spices bag, salt & water, put the lid on & pressure cook the dal over medium high flame for 3 whistles, then switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally.
Open the lid & check if the dal is cooked properly, it should be fully cooked, but it should also stay intact for the best texture, your boiled dal is ready, set it aside until you make the tempering.
Set a kadhai over high heat & let it get hot, then add the oil & ghee, let the oil get hot as well.
Further add cumin seeds & onions, stir well & cook over medium until the onion turns light golden brown.
Once the onion turn light golden brown add the garlic, ginger & green chilli, stir well & continue to cook until the onion turns completely golden brown.
Then lower the flame & add all the powdered spices, stir & immediately add a splash of hot water, stir & increase the flame & cook the spices for 3-4 minutes.
Further lower the flame again & the whisked curd, salt & stir it continuously, then increase the flame again & cook until the oil gets separated.
Once the oil gets separated, add in the boiled dal & stir well, add hot water & adjust the consistency, add plenty of hot water, it will thicken up ass the dal cooks further, stir well again & set the flame to medium & let it cook until you make the birista.
Set a deep pan over high flame & pour in the oil to fry the onions, heat the oil until it gets moderately hot.
Add the sliced onions into the oil & stir well, fry the onions until they turn light golden brown while stirring them frequently, you will need to adjust the flame as the onions starts to change their colour, make sure that the temperature is not too high or else the onions will fry from the outside but won’t lose their moisture which will result into soggy birista.
Once the onions are almost about to turn light golden brown, remove them from the hot oil using a spider & squeeze out the excess oil by pressing it using a spatula then transfer the fried onions onto a tissue paper lined plate & spread them apart using forks, dab them from the top using a tissue paper if required to remove all the remaining excess oil.
Let the fried onions rest for a couple of minutes & your birista is ready, divide it into 80% & 20%, we’ll add the larger quantity into the dal & use the remaining for garnish.
Check the dal at this stage, stir well & check its consistency.
Crush & add the birista along with fresh cream, butter, roasted kasuri methi powder & garam masala, stir well.
Taste & adjust the salt at this stage & lastly add fresh coriander.
Your dal Mughlai is ready, serve it with laccha paratha (or any Indian bread of your choice) & rice.

LALLA MUSSA DAL
Preparation time 10-15 minutes 
Cooking time 20-25 minutes  
Serve 2-4

Ingredients 
For Boiling Dal 
3 cup Chana dal, soaked, चना दाल
1/2 cup Masoor dal, soaked, मसूर की दाल
1 cup Urad dal, soaked, उड़द दाल
4-5 cups Water,  पानी
½ tsp Turmeric powder, हल्दी पाउडर
½ tsp Degi red chili powder, देगी लाल मिर्च पाउडर
Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार

For Masala
1 tsp Cumin seeds, जीरा
1 tbsp Black peppercorns, काली मिर्च के दाने
Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार
2 tsp Ghee, घी 

For Tempering 
2 tbsp Oil, तेल
2 tbsp Ghee, घी
2 medium Onion, chopped, प्याज़
4-5 Garlic cloves, chopped, लहसुन 
2-3 tbsp Curd, beaten, दही
Boiled Dal, उबली हुई दाल
Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार
1 ½ tbsp Prepared Masala, तैयार किया हुआ मसाला
1 tbsp Coriander leaves, chopped, धनिया पत्ता 
2-3 tbsp Ghee, घी (for Finishing)

For Garnish 
Coriander sprig, धनिया पत्ता

Process 
For Boiling Dal 
In a pressure cooker, add chana dal, masoor dal, urad dal, water, turmeric powder, degi red chili powder and salt to taste. 
Cover it with a lid and cook for 2-3 whistles or until the dal is cooked well. 
Keep aside for further use.
For Masala 
In a pan, add cumin seeds, black peppercorns, salt to taste, ghee and roast it well.
Transfer it to a grinder jar and grind into a fine paste. 
Keep aside for further use.

For Tempering 
In a  handi or deep kadai, add oil, ghee, once it's hot, add onion and saute for 2-4 minutes on medium flame or until golden in color. 
Add garlic cloves and saute it well. Add curd and cook well. 
Add cooked dal and mix everything well. Add salt to taste. 
With the help of a wooden spoon, mash the dal and let it cook on medium flame for 5-10 minutes or until thick. 
Add prepared masala and mix well. Add coriander leaves, finish it with melted ghee and give it a boil. 
Transfer it into a serving dish and garnish it with coriander sprig.
Serve hot with naan. 

For Finishing 
In a pan, add ghee once it's melted, keep aside for further use.

title: Sambar Recipe | Andhra Pappu Charu | Curry Recipe | Dal Rasam | Lunch Recipes | Dal Recipe I
description:
📌 Learn South Indian cooking easily 😋 👉https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLFhw-Rie62rTl2cJO7_qltIRHkFFG1lYR
Sambar Recipe | Andhra Style Pappu Charu | Curry Recipe | Dal Rasam | Dal Recipe @HomeCookingShow 

#sambarrecipe #dalrasam #andhrastylepappucharu #rasamrecipe #lunchrecipes #curryrecipe #pappucharuintelugu #telanganarecipes 

Prep Time: 10 mins
Cook Time: 45 mins
Servings: 5

To Make Andhra Style Sambar

Toor Dal - 1/2 Cup ( 125 Ml )
Tomato - 2 Nos
Garlic - 6 Cloves
Turmeric Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Oil - 1 Tbsp
Chana Dal - 1 Tsp
Mustard Seeds - 1/2 Tsp
Fenugreek Seeds - 1/4 Tsp
Cumin Seeds - 1/2 Tsp
Red Chilli - 3 Nos
Hing - 1/2 Tsp
Curry Leaves
Shallot - 15 Nos
Onion - 1 No.
Green Chilli - 2 Nos
Carrot - 1 No.
Sweet Potato - 1 No.
Radish - 1 No.
Drumsticks - 2 Nos
Salt - 2 Tsp
Red Chilli Powder - 2 Tsp
Coriander Powder - 1 Tsp
Tamarind Juice
Water - 3 Cups
Coriander Leaves

Method:

1. Wash and soak the toor dal for 30 mins in water.
2. Then transfer the soaked dal along with water into a pressure cooker. Also add in chopped tomatoes, garlic cloves, turmeric powder and salt.
3. Close the cooker with the lid and cook dal for 4-5 whistles on a medium flame to make sure it is nice and soft.
4. Now take a wide kadai and pour oil in it. Add chana dal, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, mustard seeds, red chillies, curry leaves and hing.
5. Once they are done, add shallots and mix once. Then add in the chopped regular onions.
6. After the onions are slightly cooked, add green chillies, carrot, sweet potato, radish, drumsticks and mix well.
7. Add salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder to the vegetables and mix everything well.
8. To this, add the tamarind juice and 2 cups of water.
9. Give it a quick mix, cover the kadai with a lid and cook the vegetables for 15 mins.
10. After 15 mins, add the cooked dal and tomato mixture to the kadai.
11. Mix well and add 1 more cup of water to the kadai to dilute the sambar a bit.
12. Simmer the sambar for 5 mins and once the sambar starts bubbling up, add the finely chopped coriander leaves. Mix again
13. Turn off the stove and transfer the sambar into the serving bowl to serve it hot with some steamed rice and papads/fryums by the side.

Hello Viewers,

Today we are going to see Pappu chaaru, a classic traditional telugu style lentil stew/sambar which people used to make from ages. This is basically made with lentils and vegetables as the basic ingredients. This can be made either in sweet flavour by adding a little Jaggery or in spicy flavour by skipping the Jaggery. You can prepare this stew and have it instantly with hot steaming Rice and a dollop of ghee on the top. It tastes heavenly and you will see it for yourself if you give it a try. You can also serve it with Appadams or fryums by the side. Do try it out and enjoy with your family and friends.

title: Gujarati Dal Recipe | Sweet & Tangy Khatti Meethi Dal Recipe | No Onion No Garlic Dal
description:

Gujarati Dal Recipe | Sweet & Tangy Khatti Meethi Dal Recipe | No Onion No Garlic Dal

#GujaratiDal #KhattiMeethiDal #ComfortFood #KhattiMeethiDal #GujaratiDal #NoOnionNoGarlic #IndianDal #ComfortFood #LunchRecipe #SimpleDal #SweetSourDal #GujaratiFood #HomeCooking

Full Recipe & Step-by-Step Guide:
https://homecookingshow.in/recipe/khatti-meethi-dal/

Homecookingshow Website Link:
https://homecookingshow.in/

Homecookingshow Amazon Products Link:
https://www.amazon.in/shop/homecookingshow


Khatti Meethi Dal is a traditional Gujarati dal known for its sweet and tangy flavor – a perfect comfort food for your lunch or dinner. Made without onion and garlic, this dal is simple, healthy, and perfect with rice or roti.

In this video, learn how to make authentic Gujarati-style Khatti Meethi Dal at home using basic pantry ingredients. This is a no-fuss, beginner-friendly recipe that comes together quickly.


Method


Take 1 cup of toor dal, rinse it 2–3 times, and soak it in enough water for 30 minutes. 
Once soaked, add the dal to a pressure cooker with water, ¼ tsp turmeric, and ¼ tsp salt. 
Pressure cook for 5–6 whistles on medium flame. 
Once the pressure releases, whisk the dal until smooth and creamy. Set aside.
In a pan, heat 2 tsp ghee (or oil). 
Add 1 tsp mustard seeds, 1 tsp cumin seeds, ¼ tsp fenugreek seeds, 2 tbsp peanuts, and 2 broken dry red chilies. Sauté well. 
Add ¼ tsp asafoetida, 2 slit green chilies, finely chopped ginger, and curry leaves. Next, add 1 finely chopped tomato and cook until mushy.
Pour in 1 tbsp thick tamarind extract, 1 tsp salt,  ½ tsp red chili powder,  2 tsp coriander powder,  and 2 tbsp powdered jaggery. Mix and let it melt. 
Add the cooked dal and about 1 cup water to adjust consistency. Let it simmer for 5 minutes on low flame.
Finish with chopped coriander and a dash of lemon juice. Serve hot with basmati rice.


✅ Perfect for everyday meals
✅ No onion no garlic
✅ Ideal for kids and elders
✅ Best paired with steamed rice or jeera rice

title: Dal Palak | Dal Recipes | Palak Recipes
description:
Dal Palak 
Ingredients:

Palak - 1 bunch
Toor Dal - 1/2 cup
Turmeric powder - 1/4 tsp
Oil - 1 tsp
lemon sized Tamarind (Soaked)
Green chili - 2 nos
Onions - 2 nos chopped
Tomatoes - 2 nos chopped
Chili powder - 1 tsp
Salt *only after pressure cooking

For Tempering
Ghee - 1 tsp
Oil - 1 tsp
Urad Dal - 1 tsp
Chana Dal - 1 tsp
Mustard seeds - 1/2 tsp
Cumin seeds - 1/2 tsp
Asafoetida powder - 1/2 tsp
Red chili - 2 nos
Pounded Garlic
Few Curry leaves

Method: 
1. Add water, toor dal, turmeric powder, oil, palak, tamarind (soaked), green chilis, onions, tomatoes, chilli powder in a cooker and mix well
2. Pressure cook in medium high flame
3. Now add salt and mix to the cooked dal palak base
4. In a wide pan, heat ghee and oil. 
5. Saute urad dal, chana dal, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, asafoetida powder, red chili, pounded garlic, curry leaves and mix well.
6. Now add the cooked dal palak and mix well. Done!

title: Dhaba Style Dal Tadka Aur Jeera Rice | ढाबे जैसी दाल तड़का और जीरा राइस | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe for Dal Tadka Jeera Rice

Prep time: 20-25 minutes (excluding soaking time)
Cooking time: 30-35 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Dal Tadka
Ingredients:
Boiling dal
TOOR DAL | तूर दाल 1/2 CUP
CHANA DAL | चना दाल 2 TBSP 
MASOOR DAL | मसूर दाल 2 TBSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. (SLIT)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH (JULIENNE)
WATER | पानी 2.5 CUPS (APPROX)
1st Tempering
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 1 NO. 
GINGER | अदरक 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GARLIC | लेहसुन 1 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2-3 NOS. (CHOPPED)
ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (CHOPPED)
TOMATO | टमाटर 1 NO. (CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
POWDERED SPICES
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI / DEGI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च / देगी मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1/2 TSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
WATER | पानी A SPLASH
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी हुई कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A PINCH
DRY MANGO POWDER | आमचूर पाउडर 1/2 TSP
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
2nd Tempering
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1/2 NO. (SLICED)
RED CHILLI | लाल मिर्च 1 NO. 
ASAFOETIDA | हींग 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1/2 TSP
Method:
Transfer all the three dals into a large bowl & wash them well until the water runs clear, then discard the water & add fresh water, let the dals soak for 30 minutes to an hour.
Once soaked, discard the water & transfer the dals into a cooker & add green chilli, salt, turmeric powder, julienned ginger & water, pressure cook the dal for 3-4 whistles over medium high flame.
Further switch off the flame & let the cooker de-pressurize naturally the open the lid & discard the green chilli & stir the dal well, your boiled dal is ready.
For the tadka, set a large pan over high heat & add the ghee once the pan gets hot.
Further add the cumin seeds, red chilli, ginger, garlic & green chilli & cook over medium flame for a minute.
Then add the chopped onions & cook until the onion turns light golden brown.
Further add the chopped tomato & salt, stir & cook until the tomato gets mushy then add powdered spices, stir quickly & then immediately add a lash of water & tilt the pan slight so that the flame enters the pan, do this step very carefully, doing this step will incorporate the smoky flavour without the use of any live charcoal.
Then add the boiled dal, stir well & bring it to a boil then add kasuri methi powder, dry mango powder, butter & fresh coriander & mix well.
To make the second tempering, set a small pan over high heat & once it gets hot, add the ghee.
Once the ghee gets hot add the sliced onion & fry until light golden brown, then add red chilli, asafoetida & kashmiri red chilli powder & immediately pour it over the dal.
Your dal tadka is ready.

Jeera Rice
Ingredients:
BASMATI | बासमती 1 CUP
SALT | नमक TO TASTE 
LEMON JUICE | निंबू का रस 1 TSP (ADD THE SLICE)
GHEE | घी 2 TBSP 
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Method:
Wash the rice well until the water runs clear then soak them in fresh water for 30-45 minutes.
To cook the rice, add water into a large stock pot & bring it to a roaring boil, once the water comes to a roaring boil, add salt & lemon juice (with the slice) along with the soaked rice, make sure your discard the water you soaked the rice in before adding them into the stock pot.
Cook the rice until they are 90% cooked, this will only take 4-5 minutes if the rice is soaked well.
Once cooked, transfer the rice into a sieve using a large spider then fluff them up using forks so that the excess steam escapes, cool down the rice completely.
To make jeera rice, set a wok over high heat & add ghee.
Further add the cumin seeds & once they crackle add in the boiled rice along with salt & fresh coriander.
Stir well & toss the rice over high flame for a few seconds.
Your jeera rice is ready.

title: How to make Dal Makhani at home | दाल मखनी | Festive Delights with Nutralite |Sanjeev Kapoor Khazana
description:
This festive season, treat yourself to the rich, creamy, and flavorful Dal Makhani, perfectly crafted with @Nutralite. It's the ideal addition to your festive delight menu. 

SPECIAL DAL MAKHNI

Ingredients

1½ cups whole black gram (sabut urad), soaked for 8-10 hours and drained
¼ cup red kidney beans (rajma), soaked for 8-10 hours and drained
4-5 tbsps Nutralite Doodhshakti Probiotic Butter Spread
Salt to taste
2 tsps red chilli powder
1 inch ginger, cut into thin strips
1 tsp cumin seeds
1 inch ginger, chopped
2 medium onions, chopped
1 tbsp garlic paste
1 cup fresh tomato puree
¾ cup tomato puree (tetra pack)
1 tsp garam masala powder
4 tbsps fresh cream + for garnish

Method

1. Transfer the soaked black gram and red kidney beans in a pressure cooker. Add salt, 1 tsp red chilli powder and ginger. Add 4-5 cups water and cook on medium heat till the pressure is released 6-8 times.
2. Heat Nutralite Doodhshakti Probiotic Butter Spread in a nonstick kadai. Add cumin seeds and once they start to change the colour, add ginger, onions sauté for 2-3 minutes.
3. Add garlic paste, remaining red chilli powder and mix well.
4. Add fresh tomato puree and tetra pack tomato puree and mix well. Cook on medium heat till fat separates.
5. Open the lid of the pressure cooker once the pressure settles completely and add the dal mixture to cooked tomato mixture. Mix well.
6. Add garam masala powder, fresh cream and mix well
7. Serve hot garnished with a swirl of fresh cream.

title: Sabut Moong Dal Recipe | साबुत मूंग की दाल । Whole Moong Dal Curry | Green Moong Curry,
description:
Green Moong Curry, Green Moong Dal Tadka Recipe, Sabut moong dal recipe, Green Gram Recipe,  Punjabi Sabut Moong Dal 
#SabutMoongDalTadka #GreenMoongDal #DalTadka

Ingredients for Sabut Green Moong dal

Whole Green Moong dal (साबुत हरी मूंग )  - 1/2 cup ( 10 gms ) 
Tomato (तेल )  - 1 ( 60 to 70 gms ) 
Green chili (हरी मिर्च )  - 2 
Ghee (घी )  - 2 tbsp 
Green coriander (हरा धनिया )  - 2 to 3 tbsp (finely chopped) 
Cumin seeds (जीरा )  - 1/2 tsp 
Asafoetida (हिंग )  - 1/2 pinch 
Turmeric powder (हल्दी पाउडर )  - 1/4 tsp 
Coriander powder (धनिया पाउडर )  - 1/4 tsp 
Ginger paste (अदरक पेस्ट )  - 1 tsp 
Red chili powder (लाल मिर्च पाउडर)  - 1/4 tsp 
Dry Red chilies (साबुत लाल मिर्च )  - 2 
Cloves (लौंग )  - 2 
Black peppers (काली मिर्च )  -  6 to 7 
Garam masala (गरम मसाला ) - 1/4 tsp 
Salt (नमक )  - 1 tsp ( or to taste ) 

How to make Sabut Green Moong dal

1. Take Green moong dal, washed, cleaned and soaked in water for 7 to 8 hours. put the soaked moong in the cooker to boil. Add 1.5 cup water, 1 tsp salt. Mix well. Close the lid of the cooker, cook it until a single whistle.
2. After 1 whistle, reduce flame and cook dal for 5 minutes. After 5 minutes dal is cooked, turn off flame. Keep dal in the cooker till the pressure escapes. Meanwhile shift the cooker to another flame. 
3. To make tempering, pour 2 tbsp ghee in the wok to heat. Once sufficiently hot add cumin seeds, asafoetida. Reduce flame to prevent spices from browning. Add turmeric powder, 2 whole dry red chilies, 2 cloves, 6 to 7 black pepper.
4. Saute the spices well, add 1 tsp ginger paste. Add tomato and green chilies paste, red chili powder. Roast the masala until ghee separates from it. 
5. As ghee separates from masala and it is roasted. Check the dal it is cooked and ready. If it seems dense add 1/2 cup water. Pour the boiled dal into the spices, mix well. Reduce flame. 
6. As dal is boils, add garam masala, green coriander, mix well and boil dal for 1 minute. Dal is ready.

Seving: 
7. Tempting Sabut Green Moong dal is ready. Take out the dal in the bowl. Garnish with some green coriander, pour some ghee too. Green Moong dal can be served with chapati, paratha, naan or rice.

title: Urad dhal (Black Lentil) Recipes from Sri Lanka 🫘 Indian and Sri Lankan Dosai, Sambaru & More!
description:
I’m harvesting the urad bean, also known as black gram and many other names, and will show you the journey these beans make from the field to your plate. (Or banana leaf!)
➡️ I’ll prepare today’s dishes in a traditional way: soaking and removing the skin, creating a bean paste on a stone grinder, and cooking over a wood fire on a clay stove.
Today’s menu consists of chili shrimp ulundu vadai, sambar, crispy dosa, and more!

Urad bean is related to mungo and is popular throughout South Asia.
➡️ Not only can it be used in soups like sambar, a vegetable mix with curry and tamarind, but it also goes well with sweeter foods like dosa, which is like a lentil-based pancake.
Join me for black gram-based meals that are good for your health and watch a cooking process that is also gentle on the environment!

💬 By the way, were you surprised to see that beans can be used for sweet foods or for deserts? Let me know in the comments below!

Prep Time: 10 mins
Cook Time: 30 mins
Servings: 8


Other Kheer Recipes
Royal Makhana Kheer - https://youtu.be/ti4VlRdwXkw
Caramel Vermicelli Kheer - https://youtu.be/txnTyiJ7i5s
Sooji Kheer recipe - https://youtu.be/PkXcHAjDr0s
Poha Moong Dal Kheer - https://youtu.be/RBGyLDxueUM



Moong Dal - 2 Cups ( 1 Cup - 250 Ml )
Cashew Nuts - 1/2 Cup
Raisins - 1/2 Cup
Grated Coconut - 1/2 Cup
Jaggery - 600 Gms
Milk - 1/2 Litre
Cardamom Powder - 1 Tsp
Ghee 
Water


Method:

1. Take moong dal and soak it in water for about 1 hour.
2. Drain off the water and transfer the soaked dal to a pressure cooker.
3. Pour enough water until the moon dal is fully immersed. Cover the cooker with the lid.
4. Place the weight, keep the flame on medium and cook the dal for 2 whistles. Keep it aside.
5. Take a pan and add ghee. Roast cashewnuts in it until they are golden brown in color and keep them aside.
6. Add raisins to the same pan and roast them until they are nicely plumped up. Keep them aside.
7. To the same pan, add some ghee and roast the freshly grated coconut for 5 mins on a low flame and keep it aside.
8. For the jaggery syrup, add water and jaggery to a saucepan and let the jaggery melt completely. Once done, turn off the stove, strain the jaggery and keep the syrup aside.
9. Take a large pot, pour the strained jaggery syrup and add the cooked dal. Mix well.
10. Add boiled and cooled milk and boil the milk for 5 mins.
11. After the bubbles are formed, add cardamom powder, roasted coconut, ghee, roasted cashewnuts and raisins.
12. Mix well and boil the payasam for 2 mins. 
13. Yummy moong dal kheer is ready to be served hot and nice.

Kheer is very much loved in India and it is a must have sweet for many special occasions or festivals. In this video, I have shown an interesting gooey kheer recipe with moong dal. This is easy to make and extremely tasty because moong dal is inherently a tasty dal. So watch the video till the end to know the step-by-step process and try this for any festivals or occasions. You can serve this as naivedyam for the deity and then consume it after your pooja. Try this one and let me know how it turned out for you guys in the comments section below.

Prep time: 10 minutes
Cook time: 40 minutes 
Servings: 4 

Ingredients

Rajma Beans (Soaked overnight) - 1 cup (approx.)
Butter - 1 tbsp  
Cumin powder - 1 tsp 
Pounded Ginger, Garlic & Green chili - 1 tsp
Onion - 1 no. (finely chopped)

Garam Masala - 1 tsp

Method

5. Add the cooked rajma beans and add some water.Then add amchur powder. Close and cook for about 10 minutes 
6. Add a teaspoon of sugar to balance out the flavors and finally add kasuri methi 
7. Rajma masala is ready to be served hot with some Jeera Rice or Ghee Rice

Method

First, into a pressure cooker, add the soaked dal, chopped onions, tomatoes, green chillies, garlic cloves, a small piece of tamarind, turmeric powder, salt, and red chilli powder. Pour in enough water and pressure cook it for about 4 to 5 whistles, until everything is well cooked.

Once the pressure releases, mash the cooked dal mixture thoroughly using a masher and set it aside.

Now, heat 4 teaspoons of ghee in a kadai.

Add urad dal, mustard seeds, and cumin seeds. Let them splutter.

Next, add dried red chillies, fresh curry leaves, a pinch of asafoetida, and some lightly crushed garlic cloves. Sauté everything until fragrant.

Now, add the mashed dal mixture to the pan, followed by freshly chopped greens. Mix well and let it simmer for 5 more minutes.

That’s it! Delicious Andhra style thotakura pappu is ready.

Serve it hot over steamed rice with a drizzle of ghee—comfort food at its best!

title: Quinoa Khichdi Recipe | One Pot Pressure Cooker Dish | Indian Vegetarian Moong Dal Khichri
description:
Khichdi is one of the all-time favorite comfort food. Packed with flavor and nutritious veggies, this one-pot pressure cooker meal is sure to leave you reaching for seconds!

RECIPE PAGE: https://www.patelbros.com/indian-recipes/quinoa-khichdi-in-a-pressure-cooker-easy-vegetarian-recipe

INGREDIENTS:

Khichdi
- Quinoa - 3/4 cup 
- Yellow Mung Dal - 3/4 cup
- Oil / Ghee - 2 tbsp
- Mustard Seeds - 1 tsp
- Cumin Seeds - 1 tsp
- Bay Leaf - 1 qty
- Onion - 1 qty, chopped
- Ginger - 1 inch, finely chopped
- Garlic - 3 cloves, minced
- Green Chilies - 2, minced
- Asafoetida / Hing - 1/4 tsp
- Tomatoes - 3, chopped
- Turmeric Powder - 1/2 tsp
- Red Chili Powder - 1/2 tsp
- Salt - 2 big pinches
- Pepper - to taste
- Green Beans - 1 cup, chopped
- Carrots - 1 cup, chopped
- Green Peas - 1 cup
- Water - 5 cups
- Lemon Juice - 2 tbsp
- Cilantro Leaves - 1/4 cup, chopped

Garnish
- Carrot - 1, grated
- Green Chili - 1/2, finely chopped
- Cilantro Leaves - 1 tbsp
- Lemon - 1 squeeze
- Ghee - 1tsp garnish

INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Thoroughly rinse 3/4 cup quinoa and 3/4 cup yellow mung dal under water until it runs completely clear. 
2. Transfer the quinoa and dal to a large bowl. Cover them completely with water and set them aside to soak for 30 minutes. 

PRO TIP: While soaking, prepare your other ingredients!

3. Drain the dal and quinoa after 30 minutes and set them aside.
4. In a 5 Liter pressure cooker over medium/high heat, add 2 tbsp oil or ghee. 
5. After heating, add 1 tsp mustard seeds, 1 tsp cumin seeds, and 1 bay leaf.
6. Sauté for about 1 minute until the seeds begin to crackle and the cumin turns to a darker color.
7. Add 1 chopped onion, 1 inch finely chopped ginger, 3 cloves finely chopped garlic, 2 finely chopped green chilis, and 1/4 tsp asafoetida / Hing powder.
8. Mix well and sauté for about 5 minutes, or until the onion turns translucent.
9. Add 3 chopped tomatoes, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, 2 big pinches of salt, and pepper to taste. 
10. Mix well and sauté for about 7 minutes. The tomatoes should be completely mashed. 
11. Add the soaked quinoa and dal, 1 cup chopped green beans, 1 cup green peas, 1 cup chopped carrots, and 5 cups of water. Mix thoroughly. 

PRO TIP: Do not overfill your pressure cooker. To be safe, it should not be over 2/3rds full.

12. Seal the pressure cooker, bring it to full pressure, and cook for 3 whistles.
13. After the 3rd whistle, turn off the heat and let the pressure release naturally (about 15 minutes).
14. Once finished, mix thoroughly.
15. Add 1/4 cup chopped cilantro leaves and 2 tbsp of lemon juice. Mix well.
16. Transfer Khichdi to a serving bowl. Add 1 tsp ghee, grated carrot, chopped cilantro, finely chopped green chili, and lemon to garnish. 
17. Stir well and Serve hot!

title: Arhar Dal Recipes - 3 Delicious Variations! I 3 अरहर दाल I Pankaj Bhadouria
description:
Arhar Dal Recipes - 3 Delicious Variations! I Dal Tadka, Khati Dal, Sambar I 3 अरहर दाल I Pankaj Bhadouria

Toor dal, or Arhar dak is a staple in most Indian homes. But cooking the dal the same way can be quite boring! 
So here are 3 Dals for the Whole Week- No Repetition! 
Now make your everyday meals healthier, tastier and far more interesting with three completely different dal recipes.
The Classic Dhaba Style Dal Tadka
A tangy Khatti Dal with raw mangoes
and a flavorful Vegetable Sambar.
One simple meal plan, three regional flavors or toor dal and an entire week of delicious dal combinations! 
Which one is your favorite arhar dal recipe —Dal Tadka, Khatti Dal or Sambar? Tell me in the comments.
Watch the complete video and save it for your weekly meal planning!


@EmamiHealthyTasty 

#DalRecipes #PankajBhadouria #daltadka #sambar #khattidal #mangodal #toordal #arhardal #pankajbhadouriarecipes #recipe #IndianFood #WeeklyMealPlan

Dhaba Style Dal Tadka 
Preparation Time: 30 minutes
Cooking Time: 20 minutes
Serves: 3-4
Ingredients:
1 cup Toor (arhar) Dal
¼ cup Red Masoor Dal
½ tsp Turmeric Powder
1 tsp Salt
1 tsp chopped green Chilies
1 tsp chopped Ginger
2 tsp Red Chili Powder
½ cup chopped Onions
1 tbsp chopped Garlic
1 tbsp chopped Ginger
1 tsp chopped Green Chilies
4 tbsp Desi Ghee
1 tsp Cumin Seeds
½ cup chopped Tomatoes
2 Dry Red Chilies

Method:
Pressure cook the washed dals for 3 whistles with 3 cups water, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp chopped ginger, 2 tsp chopped green chilies.
The Dals should be well cooked.
Heat 3 tbsp ghee in a pan. add 1 tsp cumin seeds. As the seeds splutter, add the chopped ginger, 1 tsp garlic and chilies. Sauté till fragrant and lightly colored. Add chopped onions and cook till the onion is lightly golden. Add the red chili powder, mix well and then add the chopped tomatoes. Cook till the tomatoes are mushy. Add the cooked dal to the pan and stir well. Cook for 2-3 minutes and remove to a serving bowl.

Sambar
Preparation time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 20 minutes
Serves: 4
Ingredients:
1 cup Arhar/ Toor Dal
2 tbsp Oil
1 tsp Mustard Seeds
10-12 Curry Leaves
3 Dry Red Chilies
1/8 tsp Asafetida
1/2 cup chopped Onions
Diced Vegetables- Brinjal, Gourd
1 + 1/2 tsp Turmeric Powder
1/2 tsp Red Chili Powder
2 tbsp Sambhar Powder
1 cup Water
1/4 cup Tamarind Pulp
Salt to taste

Method:
Soak dal for 20 minutes.
Pressure cook the toor dal with salt, turmeric and 4 cups water for 7-8 minutes or until just cooked. 
Heat the oil in a pan. Add the asafetida, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds and red chili and sauté till the mustard seeds crackle. 
Add the curry leaves and sauté for 20 seconds. 
Add the chopped onions and sauté over medium heat till the onions turn a golden color.
Add the diced vegetables, salt and sauté well. Cook till the vegetables are half done. 
Add the sambhar powder, water, cover and cook till the vegetables are almost cooked.
Now add the the dal, the tamarind pulp and cook till the raw smell of tamarind disappears. Serve hot.

Khati Aam ki dal
Preparation time: 10 minutes
Cooking time: 20 minutes
Serves: 4

Ingredients:
1 cup Dal
2 Raw mangoes
½ tsp turmeric powder
¼ tsp Hing
2 dry red chillies
½ tsp mustard seeds
½ tsp cumin seeds
Salt to taste
2 tbsp oil

Method:
Peel and slice the raw mangoes.
Soak dal for 20 minutes.
Pressure cook the toor dal with salt, turmeric and 4 cups water for 7-8 minutes or until just cooked. 
Heat the oil in a pan. Add the asafoetida, mustard seeds, cumin seeds and red chili and sauté till the mustard seeds crackle. 
Add the sliced mangoes and cook till they are tender. Add the dal and cook till the mangoes are pulpy. Serve.

2:15 Dhaba style dal Tadka

8:05 Sambar

Written recipe Dal Makhani

Prep time: 15-20 minutes (excluding soaking time)
Cooking time: 1.5 hrs
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Boiled dal
WHOLE URAD DAL | साबुत उरद दाल 1 CUP
RAJMA | राजमा 1/4 CUP
CHANA DAL | चना दाल 2 TBSP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED 
GARLIC | लहसुन 5 CLOVES
WATER | पानी 1 INCH ABOVE THE DAL
SALT | नमक 1 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
Tempering & final cooking
BUTTER | मक्खन 3 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
GINGER GARLIC PASTE | अदरक लहसुन की पेस्ट 2 TBSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 2 TBSP
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला 1/4 TSP
TOMATO PUREE | टमाटर की प्यूरी OF 5 TOMATOES 
SALT A PINCH | नमक A PINCH
HOT WATER AS REQUIRED | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED 
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम 4-5 TBSP
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी हुई कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A LARGE PINCH 
GARAM MASALA A LARGE PINCH | गरम मसाला A LARGE PINCH 
BUTTER | मक्खन 2-3 TBSP
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
Method:
Add all the three dal into a large bowl, add water & wash them by rubbing them in between your palms 3-4 times until the water added turns clear, then let the dal soak for 10-12 hours.
Once soaked wash the dal again similarly until the added water turns clear, discard that water & add the dal into the cooker.
Add garlic, water, salt & kashmiri red chilli powder & pressure cook the dal over high flame for 4 whistles, then switch off the flame & let the cooker depressurize naturally.
Further mash the dal lightly using a potato masher, don’t mash it too much to make a paste, just mash until the rajma disappears then transfer it into a bowl & set it aside until you make the tempering.
Set the same pressure cooker or a stock pot on high flame & add butter along with the oil & let the butter melt.
Further add ginger garlic paste, stir & cook over medium flame for 2 minutes.
Lower the flame & add kashmiri red chilli powder & garam masala, stir well & then add the tomato puree & salt, stir well & cook over high flame until the oil separates & the tomato puree tunrs crumbly.
Then add the cooked dal, mix well with the tempering & add hot water as required, the dal should become slightly thin so that it gets time to cook while you let it simmer further, cook the dal for at least 25 minutes until it thickens.
Once the dal has thickened after simmering for 25 minutes add fresh cream, roasted kasuri methi powder, garam masala & butter, stir well then taste for salt & adjust accordingly.
Your delicious dal makhani is ready, serve it along with some laccha parathas & rice.

description:
MEDU VADA /URAD DAL VADA - A popular breakfast or any time dish that is easily made at home too.

𝗔𝗠𝗔𝗭🥘𝗡  𝗦𝗛🍳𝗣

📟Kitchen Appliances - https://bit.ly/KitchenAppliancesIUse
***********************

𝗖𝗵𝗲𝗰𝗸 𝗼𝘂𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗶𝗽𝗲𝘀 𝘁𝗼𝗼: 
Chana Dal Vada - https://youtu.be/8CzLWsSdHv4
Hotel Jaisa Sambaar - https://youtu.be/XvvObytWWSc
Coconut Chutney - https://youtu.be/7yop_KC4kPI

𝓢𝓾𝓫𝓼𝓬𝓻𝓲𝓫𝓮 𝓱𝓮𝓻𝓮: https://goo.gl/UE2pmL

***********************
For more fantastic recipes, check out the Ranveer Brar App 📲
📲𝐀𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐨𝐢𝐝 - http://bit.ly/RBAppAndroid
📲𝗶𝗢𝗦 - http://bit.ly/RBAppiOS
***********************

MEDU VADA /URAD DAL VADA 

Ingredients
2 cups Urad dal (husked & split black gram), soaked overnight, उड़द की दाल
2-4 Green chillies, chopped, हरी मिर्च
2 tbsp Coriander leaves, chopped, धनिया पत्ती
1 tbsp Ginger, finely chopped, अदरक
1 sprig Curry leaves, chopped, करी पत्ता
¼ cup fresh Coconut pieces, chopped, नारियल
5-10  Black peppercorns, crushed, काली मिर्च
Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार
Oil for deep frying, तेल
some Water if needed to grind the batter, पानी
1 Banana leave, केला का पता
 For Coconut Chutney
½ cup fresh Coconut, grated, नारियल
1 tbsp roasted Chana dal, भूने चने की दाल
2 Green chillies, हरी मिर्च
Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार
1 tsp Cumin seeds, जीरा
4-5 Curry leaves, करी पत्ता
½ inch Ginger, slice, अदरक
2-4 Ice cube, बर्फ के टुकड़े
Water as required, for grinding, पानी

For Tempering
1 ½ tbsp Coconut oil, नारियल तेल
½ tsp Mustard seeds, राई
2 dry Red chillies, सूखी लाल मिर्च
¼ tsp Asafoetida, हींग
1 sprig Curry leaves, करी पत्ता

For Serving
Sambar, सांभर
Coconut chutney, नारियल की चटनी
Medu Vada, मेडु वड़ा

Process

In a grinder, add soaked urad dal and grind it into a smooth batter, add little water while grinding.

Transfer this mixture into the parat.

Add chopped green chillies, coriander leaves, ginger, curry leaves and fresh coconut mix it well.

Add Crushed black peppercorns, salt to taste and mix everything well.

Take a bowl of water. Apply some water on both your hands.

Take some batter in your hand and make a round shape.

With your thumb make a hole in the center. You can also use banana leaves to give a medu vada shape.

Heat oil in a kadai on medium flame.

Once the oil is hot, slide the vada into the hot oil.

Once the vada is slightly golden turn over the other side. Fry the vada on medium flame so that it’s cooked from inside.

Remove it on a kitchen tissue.

Fry them again so that it turns evenly crisp and golden in color.

Drain them on a kitchen tissue to remove excess oil.

Serve medu vada with hot sambar and coconut chutney.

For Coconut Chutney

In a bowl, add grated fresh coconut, roasted chana dal, green chili, salt to taste, cumin seeds, curry leaves, ginger and ice cubes transfer them into a grinder jar and grind into a smooth paste. Add water if required.

Transfer it into a bowl, and keep it aside.

For Tempering

Heat coconut oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, dry red chili, asafoetida and curry leaves let it splutter well.

Transfer it into a bowl and mix well.

title: Dal Vada Recipe | Monsoon Recipe | Secret Chutney | दाल वड़ा बनाने का आसान तरीका | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full Written Recipe for Dal Vada

Prep time: 15-20 minutes (excluding soaking time)
Cooking time: 20-25 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Red coconut chutney
Ingredients:
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 10-12 NOS. 
COCONUT | नारियल 1 NO. (GRATED OR SCRPAED)
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS. 
GARLIC | लेहसुन 4-5 NOS. 
ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. 
MINT | पुदीना A FEW LEAVES
TAMARIND | इमली 1 TSP
ROASTED CHANA DAL | भुनी हुई चना दाल 3 TBSP
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
Method:
Set a pan over high flame, add the oil once it gets hot followed by the kashmiri red chillies & roast them over low flame for 3-4 minutes until crisp.
Transfer the red chillies into a bowl, cool them down completely & add them into a mixer grinder jar.
Further add the fresh coconut, green chilli & all the remaining ingredients of the chutney & grind everything into a semi-coarse chutney.
Your perfect red coconut chutney is ready. 
Vada
Ingredients:
CHANA DAL | चना दाल 1 CUP
GINGER | अदरक 2 INCH
GARLIC | लेहसुन 6-8 CLOVES
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2-3 NOS.
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा ½ TSP
CORIANDER SEEDS | साबुत धनिया 1 TSP
FENNEL SEEDS | सौंफ ½ TSP
BLACK PEPPERCORNS | साबुत काली मिर्च ½ TSP
ONION | प्याज़ 1 NO. (CHOPPED)
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
CURRY LEAVES | कड़ी पत्ता 3-4 SPRIGS (CHOPPED)
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
RICE FLOUR | चावल का आटा 1 TBSP
HOT OIL | गरम तेल 1 TBSP
ASAFOETIDA | हींग A PINCH
Method:
Wash the chana dal 3-4 times in fresh water & then soak it for 7-8 hours or overnight. 
Once soaked, discard the water & add the chana dal into a mixer grinder jar, reserve 20% or a few tablespoons of the chana dal, we’ll add this later for a better texture.
Further add ginger, garlic & green chilli into the jar & grind it until you get a coarse chana dal paste, use pulse mode to grind the dal & open the jar twice or thrice to mix the ingredients for even grinding as there’s no additional water added.
Transfer the coarse paste into a large bowl, then add all the whole spices into a mortar & grind them into a coarse spice mix, you can also use a spice grinder just make sure that you don’t turn it into a fine powder.
Add the spice mix into the dal mixture followed by the reserved chana dal, chopped onion, salt, chopped curry leaves, fresh coriander, rice flour, hot oil & asafoetida, mix & combine all the ingredients with your hands to form a mixture.
To shape the vadas, take a desired portion of the mixture & form it into a roundel then press it gently between your palms to flatten it, the edges should be slightly thinner that the middle portion, that will form the crispy edge which tastes really good in a dal vada.
Shape all the vadas similarly & then carefully drop them into 165 C preheated oil, don’t stir the vadas for the initial minute or two, let them hold their shape & then stir & fry them over medium flame until crisp & golden brown.
Once fried transfer them into a sieve so that all the excess oil drips off.
Your delicious, monsoon perfect dal vadas are ready.
