title: दाल फ्राय | How To Make Restaurant Style Dal Fry | Easy Dal Fry Recipe | Madhurasrecipe
description:
Learn how to make Dal Fry - Punjabi style at home which tastes delicious and is very easy to make.

Please take a moment to like and subscribe ✿◕ ‿ ◕✿

MadhurasRecipe Marathi Whatsapp Number - 9112934976

Dal fry is a popular Indian dal or lentil recipe. This is very easy and basic recipe.  It is prepared using tur or tuval dal. But you also can use moong or masoor dal for this recipe. You can make this dal thcik or medium consistency according to your taste. Let’s learn to make dal fry and dal fry tadka.

Ingredients:
• 1 cup Tur dal
• 2 cups Water
• 1/4 tdp Turmeric powder
• A pinch of Hing / Asafoetida
• 3 tsp Butter
• 1 tsp Mustard seeds
• 1/2 tsp Cumin seeds
• 1 cup Finely chopped Onion
• 2 medium size finely chopped Tomatoes
• 2 Green chilies
• 1 1/2 tsp Coriander powder
• 1 tsp Red chili powder
• 1 tsp Garam masala (Kitchen king masala)
• Curry leaves
• 2 tsp Garlic paste
• Salt to taste
• 1 tsp Kasuri methi (optional)
For Tadka:
• 1 tsp Ghee
• 3~4 Minced Garlic cloves
• 3~4 Diced Red chilies.

Method:
• Take dal in a bowl and wash it really good 2-3 times with water. 
• Transfer the washed dal in cooker and add water.
• Add turmeric powder, hing and mix well. 
• Close the lid and cook on medium heat until 4 whistles. 
• Open the lid and beat the dal really good with beater. Keep it 
  aside. 
• Heat up butter in a pan. You can use ghee or oil instead of butter.
• When butter is a little hot, add mustard seeds and let them pop 
  up.
• Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. 
• Add finely chopped onion and fry until it turns golden. 
• Add finely chopped tomato, green chilies, coriander powder, red 
  chili powder, garam masala (kichen king masala) and garlic paste. 
• Mix everything well together. 
• Cover and cook until tomatoes become soft. 
• Open the lid and mix the masala. 
• Add the cooked dal and water to adjust the consistency. Mix well. 
• Add salt to taste and kasuri methi. Mix well. 
• Boil for about 5-7 minutes. Dal fry is already. 
• For making dal tadka, heat up ghee in a tadka pan. You can use 
  oil instead of ghee. 
• Add minced garlic and let it turn little golden. 
• Add fried red chilies and fry it. 
• Add this tadka on dal and mix well. 
• Dal fry tadka is also already. 
• This goes well with jeeraa rice.

Crispy Medu Vada is a classic South Indian breakfast recipe that is crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. This traditional vada recipe is perfect for breakfast or tiffin and pairs well with coconut chutney and sambar.




If you are looking for a medu vada recipe, South Indian breakfast or  other easy Indian tiffin ideas, you are at the right place....https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BahH5aZqllg&list=PLFhw-Rie62rSUexCVEh96_HZv5CQkSs4l&pp=sAgC






#MeduVada #CrispyMeduVada #VadaRecipes #SouthIndianVada #vada #homecooking #Garelu #uraddalvada #southindianbreakfast

Here is the link to Amazon HomeCooking Store where I have curated products that I use and are similar to what I use for your reference and purchase https://www.amazon.in/shop/homecookingshow

Prep time: 6 hrs 
Cook time: 30 mins 
Number of servings: 6-7 persons 

Ingredients 

Urad Dal - 1 cup (soaked)
Water - about 1/4 cup
Salt - 1/2 tsp
Onion - 1 no. (finely chopped)
Green Chili - 2 nos. (finely chopped)
Curry Leaves 
Oil For Deep Frying 

Method 
Soak the urad dal for 4 hrs 
Strain the water and transfer the urad dal to a mixer jar. Add the water gradually and grind it to a smooth and thick batter 
Refrigerate the batter for 2 hrs 
After 2 hrs, add salt, onions, green chilies, curry leaves to the batter and mix well 
Take a banana leaf and wipe it with some water 
Take a small amount of the batter and place it over the banana leaf and shape it (you can use water to help shape the vada) 
Heat some oil for deep frying, slowly drop the vadas into the hot oil and deep fry them till they are golden brown in color (Maintain medium flame while frying the vadas)
Serve the Medu vada hot

title: Yeh Royal Dal Recipe Aaj Tak Kyun Chhupi Rahi? | Burhanpuri Dal | Chef Ajay Chopra
description:
How to make Burhanpuri Dal | Royal, Tangy & Flavourful Traditional Dal | Chef Ajay Chopra

Subscribe to my channel here: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCjC5zJ25kXB0mwKxi4erOrg

Burhanpuri Dal is a traditional delicacy known for its rich flavours, balanced spices and comforting taste. This unique dal recipe combines simple ingredients with authentic cooking techniques to create a wholesome and satisfying meal.

Portion Serving:  4-5 people
Preparation Time: 5-10 minutes
Cooking Time: 40 minutes 

For boiling dal:
Tur dal soaked 1½ cups
Green chilli, slit 2 pcs  
Salt 1 tsp
Water as required 
Turmeric powder ½ tsp

For coconut paste mixture:
Dried coconut ¼ cup
Poppy seeds 1 tbsp
Water as required 

For spice masala mix:
Black pepper 20-25 pcs
Star anise 1 pc
Cloves 4-5 pcs 
Black cardamom 1 pc
Bay leaf 1 pc
Cinnamon 1 inch 
Green cardamom 3 pcs
Coriander seeds 1 tbsp
Sahi jeera 1 tsp
Saunf 1 tsp 

Oil 1 tbsp
Jeera 1 tsp
Bay leaf 1 pc
Cinnamon ½ inch
Onion sliced 1 pc
Ginger & garlic paste 1 tbsp
Tomato sliced 1 pc
Turmeric powder ½ tsp
Red chilli powder ½ tsp
Spice masala mix 1 tbsp
Splash of water 
Coconut paste mixture ½ cup
Add boiled dal 
Water as required 
Tamarind paste 1 tbsp

Ghee 1 tbsp
Cloves 4-5 pcs
Onion sliced 1 pc
Red chilli powder ½ tsp
Coriander chopped 1 tbsp

Step 1: Pressure cook the dal
Wash and soak toor dal.
In a pressure cooker add turmeric, salt, slit green chillies and water.
Pressure cook for about 3 whistles till soft and mushy. Keep aside. 

Step 2: Prepare coconut-poppy seed paste
Toast dry coconut pieces till nicely browned.
Dry roast poppy seeds till aromatic.
Cool both, then grind with a little water into a smooth, rich paste.

Step 3: Make spice masala mix 
Dry roast whole spices bay leaf, cinnamon, black pepper, star anise, black cardamom, green cardamom and coriander seeds first.
Then add jeera and saunf, as they roast quickly.
Cool and grind into a fragrant garam masala powder.

Step 4: Prepare first tadka base
Heat oil in a pan. Add bay leaf, cinnamon and cumin.
Add sliced onions and cook till deep golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté till aromatic.

Step 5: Cook the masala
Add sliced tomatoes and cook till soft.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder and freshly ground spice masala mix. 
Add a little water to prevent the masala from burning, and cook until it simmers.  

Step 6: Add paste & dal
Add prepared coconut-poppy seed paste and cook for a minute and mix well.
Now add cooked dal along with water to adjust consistency.
Add tamarind water and simmer till flavours blend beautifully.

Step 7: Finish the dal
Add fresh coriander and let the dal simmer for a few minutes to develop richness and depth.

Step 8: Prepare second tadka
Heat oil in a small pan. Add cloves and sliced onions.
Cook till onions turn golden, then add red chilli powder at the end.

Step 9: Final tempering & serve
Pour the hot tadka over the dal.
Serve this rich Bhuranpur-style wedding dal hot with rice or badam pulao for a royal experience

title: मेदू वडा | How To Make Crispy Medu Vada | South Indian Recipes | MadhurasRecipe
description:
Please take a moment to like and subscribe ✿◕ ‿ ◕✿
http://www.youtube.com/c/MadhurasRecipeMarathi?sub_confirmation=1

MadhurasRecipe Marathi Whatsapp Number - 9112934976

Medu vada is a very popular south Indian recipe. These are crispy from outside and soft inside. These are protein rich too. Do try medu vada and do not forget to like, comment, share and subscribe.

Ingredients:
• 1 cup Urad dal
• 2 cups Water
• Salt to taste
• Oil for frying

Method:
• Take urad dal into a bowl and wash it really good 2-3 times. 
• Add water and soak for 4 hours. 
• After 4 hours, transfer the dal into blender pot and blend into fine 
  paste. 
• Add minimum amount of water in dal while blending. 
• Blend for bout 30-40 seconds. Remove the batter at the side to 
  the center of the blender pot with the help of knife and blend 
  again.
• Repeat the process until it batter turns into fine paste.
• Take the batter out into a bowl. 
• Add salt and mix well. 
• Beat the batter for about 4-5 minutes really good to make it light 
  and fluffy. 
• Heat up oil in a pan. 
• Soak both the hands in water. 
• Take a spoonful of water in hand and make it round. 
• When it is almost round, take it on 4 fingers, make a hole at the 
  center with thumb and drop vada into oil. 
• Fry vada on medium heat only. If you fry it on too high heat, it can 
  remain raw from inside. 
• After frying for about 3-4 minutes, flip it over and from other side 
  as well. 
• Fry until it turns nice golden from both sides. 
• Take vada out of oil, drain excess oil and put it into a dish. 
• Medu vada is already. 
• You can serve medu vada with sambar and khobaryachi chutney.

title: How to make Moong Dal | #BackToBasics | Sanjeev Kapoor Khazana
description:
The basics of desi khana begins with learning the skill of making the perfect dal. Here’s the comforting Moong Dal recipe for you to make in your home kitchen.

HOMESTYLE MOONG DAL

Ingredients

1 cup split skinless green gram (moong dal)
2 tbsps oil
1 tsp cumin seeds
5-6 curry leaves
2 dried red chillies
¼ tsp asafoetida (hing)
1½ tbsps finely chopped garlic 
1 medium onion, chopped
1 medium tomato, chopped
¼ tsp turmeric powder
½ tsp red chilli powder
Salt to taste
2 tbsps chopped fresh coriander leaves
Ghee to serve
Fresh coriander sprig for garnish

Method

1. Wash split skinless green gram with sufficient water for 2-3 times, add fresh water and soak for 20 minutes.
2. To make homestyle moong dal, heat oil in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds and let them change colour.
3. Add curry leaves, dried red chillies and asafoetida and sauté for 30 seconds. Add garlic and sauté for 30 seconds. 
4. Add onion and sauté for 1 minute. Add tomatoand sauté for 1 minute.Add turmeric powder, and red chilli powder, and mix well.
5. Drain the soaked moong dal and add into the cooker. Mix well. Add salt and 3 cups water and mix well. Cover and cook under pressure till 2 whistles are given out. 
6. Open the cooker once the pressure has reduced completely, add coriander leaves and mix. Take the cooker off the heat.
7. Pour the dal into a serving bowl, drizzle ghee and garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot.

DHABA STYLE DAL FRY
Preparation Time: 10 minutes
Cooking Time: 20-25 minutes
Serves: 2

Ingredients
For Dal
2 tbsp ghee
½ cup tuvar dal, soaked
3 tbsp moong dal, soaked 
1 inch ginger, sliced 
Salt to taste
¼ tsp turmeric powder
1 tsp red chilli powder
1 green chilli
1 ½ cups water

For Tempering
1 tbsp ghee 
1 tbsp oil 
½ tsp cumin seeds
1 inch ginger, finely chopped 
½ tbsp garlic, finely chopped 
1 medium onion, finely chopped
1 tsp red chilli powder 
¼ tsp turmeric powder
Salt to taste 

For 2nd Tempering
2 tbsp ghee
2 tbsp oil 
3-4 garlic cloves, sliced 
2-3 whole dry kashmiri red chillies
A pinch of asafoetida
½ tsp kashmiri red chilli powder
Coriander leaves, for garnish

Process:-
● Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add tuvar dal and moong dal to the pressure cooker
and roast for 2-3 minutes.
● Add in ginger, salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, salt and green chilli. Mix well.
● Add water, cover and allow to cook for 3 whistles.
● Meanwhile, heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds, ginger and garlic, saute till
fragrant.

● Add onions and saute till translucent. Add chilli powder, turmeric powder and salt,
mix well. Cook for 2-3 minutes, till the rawness of the spices is gone.
● Add the boiled dal to this tempering and allow to cook for simmer for 3-4 minutes
● Meanwhile, prepare the second tempering.
● Heat ghee in a pan, add some water. Add garlic cloves, whole dry kashmiri red
chillies, asafoetida, kashmiri red chilli powder and mix well.
● Cook till it splutters.
● Pour the prepared dal into a serving dish, garnish with coriander leaves.
● Pour the prepared tempering and serve hot.
